node.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Smoky Wrap
node.short_description: Protein-rich tuna pasta salad with cucumber and cherry tomatoes tossed in olive oil; versatile for wraps or quick bowls.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve as a smoky wrap by spooning the tuna pasta salad into a whole-grain tortilla with a handful of greens, or enjoy as a ready-to-eat pasta salad in a bowl.
node.nutrition_note: Protein from tuna, complex carbohydrates from pasta, and healthy fats from olive oil provide a balanced base. Cucumber and cherry tomatoes add fiber and micronutrients. Canned tuna can be high in sodium, so consider tuna in water and drain well or control portion size to manage sodium and calories.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 6 > Child > Day 7 > Lunch > Black Bean Wrap — Smoky Bake

node.name: Black Bean Wrap — Smoky Bake
node.short_description: Smoky black bean wrap with avocado, lettuce, and salsa in a whole-wheat tortilla—fiber-rich, plant-based, and satisfying.
node.meal_suggestion: Toast the wrap for extra crispness and heat the filling until beans are warm. To boost protein, add extra black beans or sprinkle hemp seeds; for more smokiness, dust with smoked paprika or chipotle powder. Pair with a side of fruit or raw veggies to round out the meal.
node.nutrition_note: Good source of plant-based protein and fiber from black beans, plus healthy fats from avocado. Includes greens and whole grains for satiety; be mindful of sodium in store-bought salsa and wraps—opt for lower-sodium options if needed.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 6 > Child > Day 7 > Lunch > Black Bean Wrap — Garlic-Ginger Bowl

node.name: Black Bean Wrap — Garlic-Ginger Bowl
node.short_description: A whole-wheat wrap filled with black beans, avocado, lettuce, and salsa for a plant-based, fiber-rich meal.
node.meal_suggestion: Great as a quick portable lunch. For more protein, add extra beans or a side of yogurt; for a flavor twist, try a light garlic and ginger drizzle or season the beans with garlic powder and ginger during warming.
node.nutrition_note: Vegetarian wrap with protein from black beans, fiber from beans and whole-wheat wrap, and healthy fats from avocado. Estimated macros: protein 12–18 g, fiber 9–12 g, fats 9–15 g. Sodium varies with salsa; opt for low-sodium if watching salt.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 6 > Child > Day 4 > Lunch > Salmon Salad Wrap — Citrus Fresh Style

node.name: Salmon Salad Wrap — Citrus Fresh Style
node.short_description: Protein-packed salmon yogurt salad wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla with celery and greens.
node.meal_suggestion: Mix salmon with Greek yogurt and diced celery; fill a whole-wheat tortilla with greens to assemble a crunchy, protein-forward wrap. Add a splash of lemon juice for the citrus lift if desired.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich wrap with omega-3s from canned salmon and calcium from Greek yogurt. Includes fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and greens plus crunchy celery. Be mindful of sodium in canned salmon; citrus brightness can be added with a squeeze of lemon for the Citrus Fresh Style.

node.name: Turkey Sandwich — Garden Veg Combo
node.short_description: A lean turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard—protein-rich, fiber-forward, veggie-packed.
node.meal_suggestion: Great grab-and-go lunch; pair with a fruit (apple/banana) and a glass of water for a balanced meal.
node.nutrition_note: Lean protein from turkey + fiber from whole-grain bread; adds veggies (lettuce, tomato) for vitamins and fullness; mustard adds flavor with minimal fat; monitor sodium if using processed turkey or condiments.
